bio.quote: Solveig the Burner is a former woodcutter turned charcoal-burner who has lived alone in the Ashen Woods (the remains of the Greenweald) for three years. She is thin—terribly thin—with skin

system.description:
Humans

Stereotype: Quick to build, quick to die, quick to break their word.

Size: 1M; Speed: 5; Resistance: 0

novice.description:
Humans

Stereotype: Quick to build, quick to die, quick to break their word.

Size: 1M; Speed: 5; Resistance: 0

system.description: As a free action, you can open your awareness to detect supernatural creatures and phenomena. Until the end of your next turn, you know the location of any supernatural object, or any undead, construct, or creature from another world within 5 meters, even if you don't have line of effect. You know if you're detecting an item or a creature, and its nature.

novice.description: As a free action, you can open your awareness to detect supernatural creatures and phenomena. Until the end of your next turn, you know the location of any supernatural object, or any undead, construct, or creature from another world within 5 meters, even if you don't have line of effect. You know if you're detecting an item or a creature, and its nature.

system.description: Ignore temporary difficult terrain created by magic/psionics. Reduce forced movement from such sources by 1.

novice.description: Ignore temporary difficult terrain created by magic/psionics. Reduce forced movement from such sources by 1.

system.description: If frightened, slowed, or weakened, use a free action to end one condition.

novice.description: If frightened, slowed, or weakened, use a free action to end one condition.

system.description: Triggered action to take half damage from non-untyped damage (fire, cold, etc.).

novice.description: Triggered action to take half damage from non-untyped damage (fire, cold, etc.).

system.description:
A chartered frontier company that works the ash-woods and burned margins: disciplined patrols, quotas, and the grim etiquette of boundary law.

Language: Askeskogsmål

Environment: Wilderness; Organization: Bureaucratic; Upbringing: Martial

novice.description:
A chartered frontier company that works the ash-woods and burned margins: disciplined patrols, quotas, and the grim etiquette of boundary law.

Language: Askeskogsmål

Environment: Wilderness; Organization: Bureaucratic; Upbringing: Martial

system.description:
Skills: Vigilant, Vigilant, Discreet.

Languages: No additional languages.

Renown: 0; Wealth: 0; Project Points: 100

Perk: *First Blood*. Once per encounter, when you act on an ambush, pursuit, or hunt you identified first, gain an advantage (second chance) on the first related test.

novice.description:
Skills: Vigilant, Vigilant, Discreet.

Languages: No additional languages.

Renown: 0; Wealth: 0; Project Points: 100

Perk: *First Blood*. Once per encounter, when you act on an ambush, pursuit, or hunt you identified first, gain an advantage (second chance) on the first related test.

system.description: Whenever a willing ally falls and would land on you or adjacent to you, you can safely catch them as a free triggered action. Neither of you takes damage from the ally’s fall.

novice.description: Whenever a willing ally falls and would land on you or adjacent to you, you can safely catch them as a free triggered action. Neither of you takes damage from the ally’s fall.

system.description: You do not temper the heat of battle within you. You unleash it! Your experience in the wild taught you the secrets of predators, and now, like the raptor, the panther, the wolf, you channel unfettered anger into martial prowess. Primordial Chaos is your ally. Let others use finesse to clean up the wreckage left in your wake. As a fury, you devastate foes with overwhelming might, hurl yourself and enemies around the battlefield, and grow stronger as your ferocity increases. Cunning has no concept of fairness—and neither do you.

novice.description: You do not temper the heat of battle within you. You unleash it! Your experience in the wild taught you the secrets of predators, and now, like the raptor, the panther, the wolf, you channel unfettered anger into martial prowess. Primordial Chaos is your ally. Let others use finesse to clean up the wreckage left in your wake. As a fury, you devastate foes with overwhelming might, hurl yourself and enemies around the battlefield, and grow stronger as your ferocity increases. Cunning has no concept of fairness—and neither do you.

system.description:
Within the heat of battle, your determination and anger grow, fueling a Heroic Resource called ferocity.

Ferocity in Combat

At the start of a combat encounter or some other stressful situation tracked in combat rounds (as determined by the Director), you gain ferocity equal to [[lookup @hero.milestones]]{your Milestones}. At the start of each of your turns during combat, you gain 1d3 ferocity.

Additionally, the first time each combat round that you take damage, you {gain 1 ferocity}. The first time you become winded or are dying in an encounter, you {gain 1d3 ferocity}.

You lose any remaining ferocity at the end of the encounter.

Ferocity Outside of Combat

Though you can’t gain ferocity outside of combat, you can use your heroic abilities and effects that cost ferocity without spending it. Whenever you use an ability or effect outside of combat that costs ferocity, you can’t use that same ability or effect outside of combat again until you earn 1 or more Milestones or finish a extended rest.

When you use an ability outside of combat that lets you spend unlimited ferocity on its effect, such as To the Uttermost End, you can use it as if you had spent an amount of ferocity equal to your Milestones.

Growing Ferocity

You gain certain benefits in combat based on the amount of ferocity you have (see 1st-Level Aspect Features for details). These benefits last until the end of your turn, even if a benefit would become unavailable to you because of the amount of ferocity you spend during your turn.

Some Growing Ferocity benefits can be applied only if you are a specific level or higher, with the level of those benefits noted in the various Growing Ferocity tables in this section.

system.description: You can’t obtain lower than a tier 2 outcome on any Might test made to jump.

novice.description: You can’t obtain lower than a tier 2 outcome on any Might test made to jump.

system.description: You channel your ferocity into instinct and cunning, challenging the order of civilization. You have the Hide skill.

novice.description: You channel your ferocity into instinct and cunning, challenging the order of civilization. You have the Hide skill.

system.description:
You are never surprised. Additionally, whenever you would push a target with forced movement, you can slide them instead.

As your ferocity grows, you gain benefits as noted on the @UUID[Compendium.draw-steel.classes.Item.0fkMLFZHJZKpAdM6]{Reaver Growing Ferocity} table. Benefits are cumulative except where an improved benefit replaces a lesser benefit.

As your ferocity grows, you gain benefits as noted on the @UUID[Compendium.draw-steel.classes.Item.0fkMLFZHJZKpAdM6]{Reaver Growing Ferocity} table. Benefits are cumulative except where an improved benefit replaces a lesser benefit.

system.description:
You gain certain benefits in combat based on the amount of ferocity you have. These benefits last until the end of your turn, even if a benefit would become unavailable to you because of the amount of ferocity you spend during your turn. Some Growing Ferocity benefits can be applied only if you are a specific level or higher, with the level of those benefits noted in the various Growing Ferocity tables in this section.

Ferocity

Benefit

2

Whenever you use the Knockback free action, the forced movement distance gains a bonus equal to [[lookup @A]]{your Agility score}.

4

The first time you slide a creature on a turn, you .

6

You gain an advantage (second chance) on Agility tests and the Knockback free action

8 (4th Level)

The first time you slide a creature on a turn, you .

10 (7th Level)

You have a double advantage (second chance) on Agility tests and the Knockback free action.

12 (10th Level)

Whenever you use a heroic ability, you gain . Additionally, whenever you make a attribute test that imposes forced movement on a target, the forced movement distance gains a bonus equal to [[lookup @A]]{your Agility score}.

system.description: If you want a good balance of protection, speed, and damage, the Panther kit is for you. This kit increases your Toughness not by wearing armor, but through the focused battle preparation of body and mind, letting you be fast and mobile while swinging a heavy weapon at your foes
